dashboard.label: T1048.003 — DNS Exfiltration
dashboard.description: Volume-based DNS exfil detection. Aggregates Zeek dns.log; flags (src, base_domain) groups with sustained subdomain-byte rate. Complementary to T1071.004 dnscat2.

search.query: `dns_exfil_volume` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats sum(total_subdomain_bytes) AS bytes | eval bytes_human = round(bytes/1024, 1) . " KB" | table bytes_human

`dns_exfil_volume` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | eval bytes_kb=round(total_subdomain_bytes/1024, 1) | sort -total_subdomain_bytes | table window, src, base_domain, query_count, bytes_kb, avg_sub_len, max_sub_len, bytes_per_second, qtypes, sample_subdomains

index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:dns | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval _parts=split(query, ".") | eval n=mvcount(_parts) | where n>=2 | eval base_domain=mvindex(_parts, n-2) . "." . mvindex(_parts, n-1) | eval subdomain=mvindex(_parts, 0) | eval sub_len=len(subdomain) | timechart span=60s sum(sub_len) AS subdomain_bytes BY src limit=8

html.h3: T1048.003 — Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol (DNS)
html.p: DNS-exfil tools (iodine, dnsteal, custom exfilrators) encode file bytes into the leftmost label of DNS queries. Many use A records on purpose to dodge TXT-volume detections. This rule is qtype-agnostic and keys on raw subdomain byte volume per (src, base_domain).
It complements the T1071.004 dnscat2 rule rather than duplicating it: dnscat2 catches the structure (TXT + entropy + unique queries); this catches the volume. Real DNS-tunnel tools may fire both.
